text:
Athens Times

—

In its largest Air Force exercise they will take part in fighter aircraft of the 116 Battle Wing of Araxus, which is why 3,000 km. The four F-16 block 52+ adv of the 336 “OLYMPOS” War Squadron, which now reaches 28.8.2024 in the world’s most populous country, fly for the first time to distant India, sealing in the most emphatic way the strong military cooperation between Athens and New Delhi as a continuation of the excellent relationship between the two states at the highest political and diplomatic level. The four F-16 took off from Araxos to land at first phase at the “King Fasail” Air Base in Tabuk, Saudi Arabia, near the border with Jordan, in order to refuel fighters and rest the crews. The next stop for the Greek “oldies” was the Air Base “Al Dhafra” in the United Arab Emirates in the Persian Gulf. Countries with which the Air Force has worked well in recent years and maintains open channels of communication. The great 3,000 km journey was completed with the flight to India, where never before the “blue wings” of our Air Force had reached. At the Jodhpur Air Force Base are already HAF technicians and flyers and have worked with the exercise organizers so that everything is ready with the arrival of the four fighters. The Air Staff’s original intention was to involve the Air Force in “Tarang Shakti 24” with four Rafale fighters, as Indian Air Force has the same aircraft which would facilitate their support. However, the Indians informed that they would like HAF to take part in F-16 fighters, to enable them to train against aircraft that Pakistan has, a country with which India is in open confrontation and indeed very often hot incidents have been recorded in the air focusing on the Kashmir region. The Air Force responded positively to the Indian request and thus it was decided to participate in the F-16 fighter exercise of the 336 War Squadron “OLYMPOS”. The Air Force was among the first to receive an invitation to take part in “Tarang Shakti 24”, involving 30 states from around the world including the US, Japan, Great Britain and Australia. Greek “old men” will have the opportunity to co-educate with top air forces worldwide, along with some of the most modern fighters available to both the western and eastern arsenal. Characteristic is that for the first time they will fly and train with Japanese flying , who participate in the exercise with Mitsubishi F-2 fighters. It is a “Viper Zero” fighter, which is based on F-16 and manufactured by audiences from Lockheed Martin and Mitsubishi Heavy Industries. The first phase of “Tarang Shakti 24” took place in the first weeks of August, with the exercise continuing until mid-September. Our Air Force’s participation in the exercise comes as a follow-up to the excellent relationship with Indian Air Force, which first took part in the major multinational exercise “INIOCHOS 23”. The imposing Su-30MK Indian fighters landed at 117 Battle Wing and stole impressions with their presence. It was one of Indian Air Force’s few fighter presences outside India, as until then they only participated in American Red Flag and exercises organized by France and Great Britain. Source: OnAlert.gr / Kostas Sarikas

News about Greece and the World.
